description:
The image comprises four sub-panels (a, b, c, d), each presenting a thematic map of a specific geographic area, likely an Urban Atlas tile. All panels share a common colour legend located to the right of panel (d), indicating five classes: red (value 20), orange (value 53), light yellow (value 85), light green (value 118), and dark green (value 150). The values are unitless. Each panel shows a roughly rectangular region with an irregular, jagged boundary on its right and bottom edges, consistent with a geographic tile boundary. A prominent straight diagonal line divides the internal area of interest within each panel into two distinct sections, visually comparing two different geospatial datasets or processing results for the same location.

*   **Panel (a):** The left section shows a highly textured pattern in orange and light yellow. The right section, beyond the diagonal line, is predominantly solid red, adjacent to the jagged boundary, with a lighter red background.
*   **Panel (b):** The left section displays a smoother, less textured pattern compared to panel (a), still primarily in orange and light yellow. The right section remains predominantly red.
*   **Panel (c):** The left section continues to show patterns in orange and light yellow, but the right section has transitioned to a blend of light yellow and light green.
*   **Panel (d):** The left section retains orange and light yellow hues. The right section now appears predominantly in dark green and light green, indicating a significant shift towards higher values according to the legend, suggesting an increase in the measured parameter.

The progression from panel (a) to (d) likely illustrates different outputs or processing stages related to urban change detection, possibly integrating Very High-Resolution (VHR) imagery and Sentinel-2 time series data, as mentioned in the surrounding context. The diagonal split serves to highlight differences or comparisons between data sources or methodologies within the specified tile.
